meta.reasoning: 4c-9s offsuit is a weak starting hand with no straight or flush potential. Folding pre-flop is the correct play in UTG position.

A5o on the button is a strong raise hand. With s4 folded, only 2 opponents remain. Raising 3x BB to 60 applies pressure, takes advantage of position, and can take the pot pre-flop or set up a favorable post-flop situation with the ace.

meta.reasoning_summary: Holding 8d Qc in small blind. S1 raised to 60 (3x BB). Marginal hand out of position — folding is correct.

meta.reasoning: Q9 offsuit in BB vs button raise. s1 is on the button with a wide stealing range. Pot odds of 2.25:1 justify a call to see the flop. Only 40 chips at risk with 980 behind.

meta.reasoning_summary: Pair of 9s (9c + 9d on board) on flop 7d 9d 4s. No current bet, acting first. Value bet ~half pot (65) to build pot and extract value from s1 who raised pre-flop.

result.summary: s3 wins by chip lead after 30 hands (s3: 2120, s4: 1880). Biggest pot: 1770. Showdowns: 9.
